pg14-p1: to establish and maintain the new order of things, were the essential requirements.
pg14-p2-s1: Simple, but not easy; a price had to be paid.
pg14-p2-s3:
I must turn in all things to
the Father of Light who presides over us all.
pg14-p2:
Simple, but not easy; a price had to be paid. It meant destruction of self-centeredness. I must turn in all things to
the Father of Light who presides over us all.
pg14-p3-s1: These were revolutionary and drastic proposals, but the moment I fully accepted them, the effect was electric.
pg14-p3-s2:
There was
a sense of victory, followed by such a peace and serenity as I had never known.
pg14-p3-s4:
I felt
lifted up, as though the great clean wind of a mountain top blew through and through.
pg14-p3-s5:
God comes to most men gradually,
but His impact on me was sudden and profound.
pg14-p3:
These were revolutionary and drastic proposals, but the moment I fully accepted them, the effect was electric. There was
a sense of victory, followed by such a peace and serenity as I had never known. There was utter confidence. I felt
lifted up, as though the great clean wind of a mountain top blew through and through. God comes to most men gradually,
but His impact on me was sudden and profound.
pg14-p4-s1: For a moment I was alarmed, and called my friend, the doctor, to ask if I were still sane.
pg14-p4-s2:
He listened in wonder as I
talked.
pg14-p4:
For a moment I was alarmed, and called my friend, the doctor, to ask if I were still sane. He listened in wonder as I
talked.
pg14-p5-s1: Finally he shook his head saying, "Something has happened to you I don't understand.
pg14-p5-s2: But you had better hang on to it.
pg14-p5-s3: Anything is better than the way you were."
pg14-p5-s4: The good doctor now sees many men who have such experiences.
pg14-p5-s5:
He knows that
they are real.
pg14-p5:
Finally he shook his head saying, "Something has happened to you I don't understand. But you had better hang on to it. Anything is better than the way you were." The good doctor now sees many men who have such experiences. He knows that
they are real.
pg14-p6-s1:
While I lay in the hospital the thought came that there were thousands of hopeless alcoholics who might be glad to have
what had been so freely given me.
pg14-p6-s2: Perhaps I could help some of them.
pg14-p6-s3: They in turn might work with others.
pg14-p6:
While I lay in the hospital the thought came that there were thousands of hopeless alcoholics who might be glad to have
what had been so freely given me. Perhaps I could help some of them. They in turn might work with others.
pg14-p7-s1: My friend had emphasized the absolute necessity of demonstrating these principles in all my affairs.
pg14-p7-s2:
Particularly was it
imperative to work with others as he had worked with me.
pg14-p7-s3: Faith without works was dead, he said.
pg14-p7-s4:
And how appallingly true
for the alcoholic!
pg14-p7-s5: For if an alcoholic failed to perfect and enlarge his
